name: Gudrun
description: Gudrun, a prominent figure in Norse heroic legend, is a complex character defined by immense suffering, vengeance, and resilience. She is primarily known from the Poetic Edda and the Völsunga saga, where her life is marked by tragedy and loss. Gudrun is a princess, often depicted as the daughter of Gjuki, king of the Burgundians. Her story is one of forced marriages, betrayals, and the relentless pursuit of retribution for the wrongs committed against her and her kin. She endures the deaths of multiple husbands and children, often at the hands of those she trusted or was forced to align with. Gudrun's character showcases the brutal realities of the Viking Age, reflecting themes of honor, revenge, and the cyclical nature of violence. Despite enduring extreme adversity, she maintains a fierce spirit and ultimately exacts vengeance on those who wronged her, solidifying her place as a powerful and enduring figure in Norse mythology. Her later marriages and actions are often interpreted as a strategic effort to regain power and influence after devastating losses.
subtitle: The Vengeful Queen

keyMyths.title: The Death of Sigurd
keyMyths.description: Gudrun's first husband, the hero Sigurd, is murdered by her brothers under the influence of Brynhild. Gudrun's grief is immense, and she is unable to weep until she is shown Sigurd's pierced eyes, which finally releases her sorrow. This event sets the stage for her future actions, as she becomes consumed by a desire for revenge against those responsible for Sigurd's death, particularly her brothers and Brynhild. The betrayal and loss deeply traumatize her, influencing her subsequent decisions and relationships.
keyMyths.source: The Poetic Edda, Sigrdrífumál and Guðrúnarkviða I

keyMyths.title: Gudrun's Marriage to Atli
keyMyths.description: After Sigurd's death, Gudrun is forced to marry Atli, the king of the Huns, against her will. Atli, driven by greed for the Nibelung treasure, invites Gudrun's brothers, the Burgundians, to his court with the intention of killing them and claiming their wealth. Gudrun, knowing Atli's intentions, warns her brothers, but they ultimately fall victim to his treachery. This betrayal leads to Gudrun's ultimate revenge against Atli, showcasing her strategic cunning and fierce determination.

keyMyths.title: The Revenge on Atli
keyMyths.description: To avenge her brothers' deaths, Gudrun murders her and Atli's two sons and serves their cooked flesh to Atli at a feast. After he is drunk, she sets fire to the hall, killing Atli and his entire court. This act of extreme violence demonstrates the depth of Gudrun's grief and her unwavering commitment to revenge. The story highlights the destructive nature of vengeance and the devastating consequences of betrayal in Norse heroic legend. The act is one of the most brutal in the Norse sagas.

keyMyths.title: Gudrun and Jonakr's Sons
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Gudrun later marries Jonakr and has three sons with him. These sons become involved in a feud with Svanhild, Sigurd's daughter, leading to her brutal execution. Gudrun, despite her past tragedies, urges her sons to avenge Svanhild's death, further emphasizing her commitment to justice and revenge. This myth demonstrates the cyclical nature of violence and the enduring consequences of past actions in the Norse sagas.

sources.author: Ursula Dronke
sources.work: The Poetic Edda, Volume I: Heroic Poems
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the heroic poems of the Poetic Edda, including those featuring Gudrun.

sources.author: Hilda Ellis Davidson

sources.description: A general overview of Norse mythology, providing context for understanding Gudrun's role within the broader mythological framework.

sources.author: John Lindow

sources.description: An accessible guide to Norse mythology, offering insights into the cultural significance of Gudrun and her story.
